source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two strings of force constant 0.5 N/ m are attached on the opposite sides of a wooden block of mass 0.01 kg. To the wooden piece is fixed a pointer which touches a vertically moving plane paper. The wooden piece kept on a smooth horizontal table is now displaced by 0.02 m along the line of springs and released. If the speed of paper is 0.1 m/ s,find the distance between two consecutive maximas on this path.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate nontrivial visual reliance in dataset annotation
image_manifest contains 1 valid image asset and registry_passed=true
The setup is apparatus-based (springs, block, pointer, moving paper), which is commonly supported by a diagram
However, the question text itself states the main physical arrangement and numerical values, so image dependence is not maximal
multi_step: Solving requires combining the two spring constants into an effective restoring constant for the block
Then the SHM time period must be computed from mass and effective spring constant
The final distance between consecutive maxima on the paper requires linking oscillation period with paper speed
This is a deterministic multi-stage physics calculation rather than a direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text is a specific numeric value with unit
choices provide a matching option, enabling straightforward answer checking
All needed numerical inputs are explicitly given in the question text
The result can be independently verified by standard SHM formulas and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产均已齐全，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空，核心语义支撑完整
综合质量较好：multi_step_score=0.74、verifiability_score=0.94，具有明确可验证的物理计算链条
虽存在 image_need_ambiguous_text_may_be_sufficient 风险，但评分证据表明题干已给出主要物理设置和数值，样本仍然可用
numeric_answer_may_have_equivalent_unit_formats 只影响答案归一化校验，不构成拒绝或降级的核心缺陷
